news.Title: IBM Consulting Rebounds Slightly: Is AI Enough to Fuel Durable Growth?
news.Content:
International Business Machines Corporation IBM is witnessing early signs of recovery in the Consulting segment. During the fourth quarter, IBM reported revenues of $5.35 billion compared with $5.17 billion a year ago, with increased demand for AI services, growth in business application transformation, application modernization and migration, and application operations. Segment profit increased to $658 million from $606 million for respective margins of 12.3% and 11.7%.

Businesses are moving from experimentation to enterprise-scale AI deployment. IBM Consulting boasts comprehensive industry-specific capabilities that are helping businesses execute their AI adoption initiatives. Per a report from Future Market Insights, the AI consulting services market is projected to witness a 26.2% compound annual growth rate between 2025 and 2035. IBM is expanding its portfolio with innovation and strategic collaboration to capitalize on this market trend.

IBM has formed a collaboration with Microsoft to augment its consulting capabilities. IBM is integrating Microsoft’s Copilot with IBM’s multi-model, multi-framework AI platform, IBM Consulting Advantage, to boost the efficiency of IBM consultants. The company is also collaborating with NVIDIA. By incorporating NVIDIA's GPU-native technologies with IBM’s watsonx.data, watsonx.ai and storage solutions, IBM aims to accelerate enterprise AI adoption. Such initiatives bode well for sustainable growth.

However, growing geopolitical unrest and global macroeconomic headwinds can impact its growth prospects. These factors can constrain enterprise decisions on AI-related spending in the near term.

How Are Competitors Faring?

IBM faces competition from Accenture ACN and Infosys Technologies INFY in the consulting space. During the recent quarter, Accenture’s Consulting revenues increased 4% year over year on a reported basis and 3% in local currency to $9.4 billion. Consulting bookings were $9.9 billion. Growing spending on application modernization and maintenance, cloud enablement and cybersecurity-as-a-service boasts a solid growth opportunity for Accenture.

Infosys’ revenues increased 3.2% year over year to $5.1 billion in the recent quarter. It is expanding its gen AI portfolio to capitalize on the growing opportunities in this domain. The company is collaborating with industry leaders, such as Google and Microsoft, to help organizations develop AI native solutions. Infosys is acquiring Optimum Healthcare IT and Stratus to build industry-specific consulting capabilities.

Story Continues

IBM’s Price Performance, Valuation and Estimates

IBM stock has declined 4.6% over the past year against the industry’s growth of 91.6%.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Going by the price/earnings ratio, IBM’s shares currently trade at 18.83 forward earnings, above 9.3.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for IBM’s earnings for 2026 and 2027 has risen over the past 60 days.

IBM currently carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Title: Google warns quantum attack could crack Bitcoin in 9 minutes
news.Content: After announcing a 2029 target for migration to post-quantum cryptography (PQC), Google has warned that a quantum system could attack a Bitcoin (BTC) transaction in about nine minutes. The tech giant warned it won't happen today but it's a possibility one should be prepared for sooner than later. Related: Google reveals major 2029 quantum threat for digital wallets Understanding quantum threat to cryptocurrency Cryptocurrencies are virtual currencies that rely on ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Google warns that quantum computers could crack Bitcoin transactions in approximately nine minutes.
• The company targets 2029 for migration to post-quantum cryptography (PQC).
• Quantum computers use qubits, which can represent combinations of ones and zeros simultaneously, offering massive computing power.
• Google's research suggests fewer qubits are needed to crack cryptocurrency cryptography than previously thought.
news.Keywords: Quantum Computing, Cybersecurity Threat, Cryptocurrency, Bitcoin, Post-Quantum Cryptography

news.Title: ANET's Margin Expansion Moderates: Can Growth Be Sustained?
news.Content:
Arista Networks Inc. ANET reported a non-GAAP operating income of $3.85 billion in 2025, up from $2.94 billion in 2024. Non-GAAP operating margin improves to 48.2% from 47.5% a year ago.

The company’s operating margin continues to be driven by healthy demand in the AI networking market. Its software native architecture, which includes a unified software stack across various use cases, including WAN routing, campus and data center infrastructure, sets it apart from its competitors. This ensures lower incremental operating costs per dollar of revenue and ensures better economies of scale.

Growing demand for 400G, 800G switches and adoption of AI networking architecture by hyperscalers across data centers are also driving growth. However, the company witnessed a significant rise in operating expenses as well. Research & development costs rose to $348.4 million from $285 million. Sales and marketing expenses also increased to $139.1 million from $111 million due to a rise in headcount and new product introduction costs. These growing expenditures partially reverse the positive trend.

Accounts payable day was 66 days up from 55 days in the first quarter. This actually improves cash flow and also improves liquidity, allowing Arista to invest more in research and development and marketing without putting too much pressure on operating margin. However, the company is highly exposed to hyperscalers’ AI spending. Growing geopolitical unrest and macro headwinds can hinder the AI spending decision and hinder the company’s operating margin growth.

How Are Competitors Faring?

Arista faces competition from Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company HPE and Cisco Systems, Inc. CSCO. HPE reported a non-GAAP operating profit of $1.18 billion compared with $780 million in the year-ago quarter. The non-GAAP operating margin improved to 12.7%, up 280 basis points from the year-ago quarter. HPE is benefiting from a robust demand environment for its edge-to-cloud platform, GreenLake, as customers are undergoing a major digital transformation.

In the second quarter of fiscal 2026, Cisco reported total non-GAAP operating expenses of $5.04 billion, up 6.2% year over year. As a percentage of revenues, operating expenses declined 110 bps year over year. Consequently, Cisco reported a non-GAAP operating income of $5.31 billion, up 9.3% year over year. Operating margin contracted 10 bps year over year to 34.6%. Despite recent contraction, strong demand for AI infrastructure and security solutions is expected to drive the company’s margin in the upcoming quarters.

Story Continues

ANET’s Price Performance, Valuation & Estimates

Shares of Arista have surged 47.9% over the past year against the industry’s decline of 13.3%.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

From a valuation standpoint, Arista trades at a forward price-to-sales ratio of 12.36, above the industry average.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for Arista’s earnings for 2026 has increased over the past 60 days.

Arista currently carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Title: Micron Technology Is Having Its Nvidia Moment. Is It Still a Buy?
news.Content: A couple of years ago, when Nvidia began churning out quarters of triple-digit revenue growth, it almost seemed too good to be true. In hindsight, it was the beginning of the artificial intelligence (AI) boom, and investors who stayed on the sidelines probably wish they ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) is experiencing a demand surge for its high-bandwidth memory (HBM), similar to Nvidia's GPU boom.
• Micron's sales exploded to over $23.8 billion for the quarter, up from $8 billion a year ago.
• The company can currently meet only half to two-thirds of customer demand, indicating a growing backlog.
• Shares trade at less than 7 times 2026 earnings estimates, with analysts expecting 90% annual earnings growth over five years.
news.Keywords: HBM Demand, Memory Supercycle, Earnings Growth, AI Infrastructure, Semiconductor Supply

news.Content: NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ: NVDA ) is one of the High Growth Semiconductor Stocks to Buy . On March 19, Reuters reported that NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) has ...

news.Title: Can Ericsson's Network Enhancement Deal With SoftBank Aid Its Shares?
news.Content:
Ericsson ERIC has strengthened its long-standing partnership with SoftBank Corp by signing a long-term agreement to upgrade and expand its core network in Japan. The alliance highlights Ericsson’s increasing importance in cloud-based 5G technology and its role in building advanced telecom systems.

Per the deal, Ericsson will deploy its dual-mode 5G Core solution, built on its Cloud Native Infrastructure Solution. This platform allows SoftBank to seamlessly integrate both 4G and 5G services while accelerating its transition to 5G Standalone and improving network performance, reliability and service quality by deploying solutions, such as User Data Consolidation, policy control systems and cloud-based IP Multimedia Subsystem.

Ericsson’s modernization effort also includes upgrading subscriber data management with cloud-native architecture and enhancing IP Multimedia Subsystem capabilities to improve voice and multimedia services, while using automation and cloud technologies to simplify operations, reduce costs, speed up new service launches and lower energy consumption.

As telecom operators adopt cloud-based and AI-driven networks, Ericsson is playing an important role in shaping the future of connectivity. With this deal, the company will help accelerate 5G growth in Japan while reinforcing its importance in driving innovation and efficiency in the telecom industry.

How Are Competitors Faring?

Ericsson faces stiff competition from Nokia Corporation NOK and Cisco Systems, Inc. CSCO. Nokia is expanding its 5G business by signing new network deals and upgrading telecom infrastructure in different countries. It is also focusing on artificial intelligence (AI)-driven networks through partnerships with companies like NVIDIA Corporation NVDA, aiming to develop advanced 5G and future 6G technologies.

Cisco is focusing on AI-powered network solutions to help telecom companies manage growing data demand. It is also working with Reliance Jio, AMD and Nokia to build smarter telecom networks. The company has also launched a new AI-based chip, Silicon One G300, to improve network performance and support the growing AI market.

ERIC’s Price Performance, Valuation & Estimates

Ericsson shares have gained 42.1% over the past year compared with the industry’s 42.8% growth.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

From a valuation standpoint, Ericsson trades at a forward price-to-sales ratio of 1.44, below the industry tally of 4.68.

Earnings estimates for 2026 have increased 3% to 68 cents over the past 60 days, while the same for 2027 have increased 3% to 71 cents.

Story Continues

Ericsson currently carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Keywords: Earnings Outlook, Financial Projections, Analyst Estimates, Valuation Concerns, Growth Backlog

news.Title: Trump Tax Law’s Affordable Housing Boost Hits Snag
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- The US government’s largest affordable housing program was supposed to be supercharged by President Donald Trump’s signature tax law. So far, that jolt hasn’t arrived. That’s because, investors and developers say, their efforts to take advantage of the dramatic expansion of the Low-Income Housing Tax Credit have bumped up against some unintended consequences. In particular, the law increased the potential size of any given affordable housing development deal eligible for the credit – but that, in turn, means few banks are able to finance them under the complex rules of the program. “The market has slowed pretty ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The US government's affordable housing program faces challenges due to unintended consequences of a tax law.
• Banks face limitations on financing large affordable housing deals due to caps on 'public welfare investments'.
• Proposed legislation aims to raise these caps, but its passage is uncertain.
• The lack of financing is stalling the development of new affordable housing units.
news.Keywords: Affordable Housing, Tax Credits, Financial Regulations, Housing Development, Investment Caps

news.Content: （中央社記者林尚縈柏林2026年03月31日專電）台積電德國廠近日舉行居民座談會說明建廠進度，居民關注施工帶來的交通與環境影響，要求廠方投入地方基礎建設。歐積電總裁表示，願盡力改善施工對 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台積電德國廠（ESMC）近日舉辦居民座談會，當地居民主要關注施工帶來的交通與環境影響。
• 居民要求廠方投入地方基礎建設，但台積電總裁表示，建廠資金用途受限，無法承擔公共基礎建設責任。
• 廠方將設置雨水滯洪池，並表示將創造約2000個高薪就業機會，支持當地文化與教育項目。
• 台積電持股ESMC 70%，其餘股東為博世、英飛凌與恩智浦各持10%。

news.Title: 4 Cloud Computing Stocks to Buy Amid Heightened Market Volatility
news.Content:
An updated edition of the Feb. 10, 2026 article.

With improved flexibility, scalability, cost savings and accessibility, cloud computing has led to widespread adoption. Driving innovation and digital transformation by capitalizing on virtualization technology, it has enabled users to access and store data over the Internet without managing physical servers and intricate IT infrastructure. This has facilitated the development of cutting-edge technologies, enabling multiple users to share the same hardware resources by connecting to the cloud platform through a web browser or dedicated applications. This, in turn, has created the framework for seamless omnichannel customer engagement at lower costs.

Cloud computing has emerged as an attractive theme for investors seeking to invest in blue-chip tech firms. This has made cloud computing firms such as Alphabet Inc. GOOGL, Microsoft Corporation MSFT, Amazon.com, Inc. AMZN and International Business Machines Corporation IBM indispensable to any investment portfolio. But before digging deep into these prized possessions, let us examine a little more into why organizations are increasingly adopting cloud computing.

Based on a pay-per-use pricing model, enterprises only pay for the computing resources they use. This has helped business enterprises to reduce operating costs for maintaining on-site data centers and deploying IT experts to manage the infrastructure, making it a highly cost-effective solution. With easy access to a plethora of innovative technologies, cloud computing increases productivity with greater agility and flexibility, and improves scalability with higher economies of scale. Moreover, cloud computing services are delivered over a highly secure network with low latency for applications and data backup facilities for improved reliability.

Cloud computing services fall into four broad categories, namely infrastructure as a service (IaaS), platform as a service (PaaS) and serverless and software as a service (SaaS), offering different levels of control, flexibility and management options to business enterprises. Cloud computing, which relies heavily on virtualization and automation technologies, provides the requisite infrastructure for AI (artificial intelligence) and machine learning (ML) workloads. It delivers powerful computing abilities to process and analyze data, creating an ideal platform for Big Data management.

Per Grand View Research, the global cloud computing market size is expected to swell to $3,349.6 billion by 2033 from $943.7 billion in 2025 at a CAGR of 16% with a variety of capabilities across multiple industries. These include diverse use cases such as improved patient monitoring and outcomes in healthcare, personalized financial management and predictive spending, immersive learning in education, superior inventory management in retail and predictive maintenance and better supply chain management in the manufacturing sector.

If you intend to capitalize on this buzzing trend, our Cloud Computing Thematic Investing Screen could make it easy to identify high-potential stocks in this domain at any given time, just like the four mentioned above. By leveraging advanced tools, our thematic screens identify companies shaping the future, making it easier to benefit from emerging trends.

Ready to uncover more transformative thematic investment ideas? Explore 36 cutting-edge investment themes with Zacks Thematic Investing Screens and discover your next big opportunity.

Story Continues

Key Cloud Computing Stocks to Watch

Alphabet has been growing rapidly in the booming cloud-computing market. Over the last few years, the company has evolved from primarily being a search engine provider to a leading provider of cloud computing technology. Google Cloud is one of the key catalysts behind the company’s growth, driven by its strengthening cloud service offerings.

The solid adoption of the Google Cloud Platform and Google Workspace and continued investments in infrastructure, security, data management, analytics and AI have helped Google to expand its cloud footprint worldwide. The increasing number of cloud regions and availability zones globally has been a hallmark of Google Cloud. Currently, Google Cloud has 43 cloud regions, 130 availability zones and more than 200 network edge locations across more than 200 countries. Google Cloud is considered the third-largest cloud player among numerous cloud providers worldwide.

Alphabet’s growing GenAI capabilities and significant investments in cloud computing are potential catalysts for the future amid stiff competition in the cloud space and increasing regulatory headwinds. It has a VGM Score of B. Alphabet carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

Microsoft is one of the most prominent public cloud providers that delivers a wide variety of IaaS and PaaS solutions at scale. Microsoft Azure, its cloud computing platform, allows users to build, run and scale applications in the cloud. It offers a variety of services, including storage, networking, analytics and AI.

Microsoft has doubled down on the cloud computing opportunity. Azure’s increased availability in more than 60 announced regions globally has strengthened Microsoft's competitive position in the cloud computing market. Operating through a vast network of global data centers that ensure high availability and reliability for applications, Azure offers seamless access to all the services included in the portal once customers subscribe to it. Subscribers can use these services to create cloud-based resources, such as virtual machines (VMs) and databases, which can then be assembled into running environments used to host workloads and store data.

As Microsoft continues to push the boundaries of networking technology, it aims to create innovative, resilient and secure solutions that enable businesses to leverage AI and the cloud to their fullest potential. This Zacks Rank #3 stock is investing heavily in AI-powered cloud services, integrating Azure OpenAI Service, Copilot and ML into various cloud solutions, making AI a central feature of Azure to empower organizations to manage their applications with greater confidence and efficiency.

Amazon enjoys a leading position in the cloud-computing market, particularly in the IaaS space, thanks to Amazon Web Services (AWS), which is one of its high-margin-generating businesses. The expanding customer base of AWS, driven by its strengthening cloud offerings, will continue to aid Amazon's dominance in the global cloud space.

AWS is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted on-demand cloud computing platform, offering more than 200 fully featured services from data centers globally. Millions of customers, including the fastest-growing startups, largest enterprises and leading government agencies, are using AWS to lower costs, become more agile and innovate faster. It reportedly offers the widest variety of databases that are purpose-built for different types of applications to enable subscribers to choose the right tool for the job.

Amazon aims to extend AWS’ AI and ML capabilities to facilitate improved decision-making. This Zacks Rank #3 stock intends to expand its global infrastructure for faster and more reliable service with low latency and maximum availability. From cloud-native applications and AI-driven solutions to edge computing and sustainability initiatives, AWS is likely to push the limits in the realm of cloud computing technology.

IBM has gradually evolved as a provider of cloud and data platforms. The Red Hat acquisition, in particular, has helped it strengthen its competitive position in the hybrid cloud market. With the buyout, the company offers a Linux operating system — Red Hat Enterprise Linux — and a hybrid cloud platform — Red Hat OpenShift — aiding enterprises with digital transformation.

With a surge in traditional cloud-native workloads and associated applications, along with a rise in generative AI deployment, there is a radical expansion in the number of cloud workloads that enterprises are currently managing. This has resulted in heterogeneous, dynamic and complex infrastructure strategies, which have led firms to undertake a cloud-agnostic and interoperable approach to highly secure multi-cloud management, translating into a healthy demand for IBM hybrid cloud solutions. In addition, the buyout of HashiCorp has significantly augmented IBM’s capabilities to assist enterprises in managing complex cloud environments. HashiCorp’s tool sets complement IBM Red Hat’s portfolio, bringing additional functionalities for cloud infrastructure management and bolstering its hybrid multi-cloud approach.

IBM is poised to benefit from strong demand for hybrid cloud and AI, driving growth in the Software and Consulting segments. This Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) stock’s growth is expected to be aided primarily by analytics, cloud computing and security in the long run.

Microsoft is one of the most prominent public cloud providers that delivers a wide variety of IaaS and PaaS solutions at scale. Microsoft Azure, its cloud computing platform, allows users to build, run and scale applications in the cloud. It offers a variety of services, including storage, networking, analytics and AI.

Microsoft has doubled down on the cloud computing opportunity. Azure’s increased availability in more than 60 announced regions globally has strengthened Microsoft's competitive position in the cloud computing market. Operating through a vast network of global data centers that ensure high availability and reliability for applications, Azure offers seamless access to all the services included in the portal once customers subscribe to it. Subscribers can use these services to create cloud-based resources, such as virtual machines (VMs) and databases, which can then be assembled into running environments used to host workloads and store data.

As Microsoft continues to push the boundaries of networking technology, it aims to create innovative, resilient and secure solutions that enable businesses to leverage AI and the cloud to their fullest potential. This Zacks Rank #3 stock is investing heavily in AI-powered cloud services, integrating Azure OpenAI Service, Copilot and ML into various cloud solutions, making AI a central feature of Azure to empower organizations to manage their applications with greater confidence and efficiency.

Amazon enjoys a leading position in the cloud-computing market, particularly in the IaaS space, thanks to Amazon Web Services (AWS), which is one of its high-margin-generating businesses. The expanding customer base of AWS, driven by its strengthening cloud offerings, will continue to aid Amazon's dominance in the global cloud space.

AWS is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted on-demand cloud computing platform, offering more than 200 fully featured services from data centers globally. Millions of customers, including the fastest-growing startups, largest enterprises and leading government agencies, are using AWS to lower costs, become more agile and innovate faster. It reportedly offers the widest variety of databases that are purpose-built for different types of applications to enable subscribers to choose the right tool for the job.

Amazon aims to extend AWS’ AI and ML capabilities to facilitate improved decision-making. This Zacks Rank #3 stock intends to expand its global infrastructure for faster and more reliable service with low latency and maximum availability. From cloud-native applications and AI-driven solutions to edge computing and sustainability initiatives, AWS is likely to push the limits in the realm of cloud computing technology.

IBM has gradually evolved as a provider of cloud and data platforms. The Red Hat acquisition, in particular, has helped it strengthen its competitive position in the hybrid cloud market. With the buyout, the company offers a Linux operating system — Red Hat Enterprise Linux — and a hybrid cloud platform — Red Hat OpenShift — aiding enterprises with digital transformation.

With a surge in traditional cloud-native workloads and associated applications, along with a rise in generative AI deployment, there is a radical expansion in the number of cloud workloads that enterprises are currently managing. This has resulted in heterogeneous, dynamic and complex infrastructure strategies, which have led firms to undertake a cloud-agnostic and interoperable approach to highly secure multi-cloud management, translating into a healthy demand for IBM hybrid cloud solutions. In addition, the buyout of HashiCorp has significantly augmented IBM’s capabilities to assist enterprises in managing complex cloud environments. HashiCorp’s tool sets complement IBM Red Hat’s portfolio, bringing additional functionalities for cloud infrastructure management and bolstering its hybrid multi-cloud approach.

IBM is poised to benefit from strong demand for hybrid cloud and AI, driving growth in the Software and Consulting segments. This Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) stock’s growth is expected to be aided primarily by analytics, cloud computing and security in the long run.

news.Title: Marvell Technology Soars on Nvidia's $2B Investment. Should You Buy MRVL Stock Here, Too?
news.Content:
Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares surged more than 8% this morning after Nvidia (NVDA) announced a $2 billion direct investment in the company alongside a sweeping strategic partnership. The deal integrates Marvell into Nvidia's AI factory and AI-RAN ecosystem through the NVLink Fusion platform, a rack-scale architecture that enables customers to build semi-custom AI infrastructure using Nvidia's NVLink ecosystem. Under the agreement, Marvell will supply custom XPUs and NVLink Fusion-compatible scale-up networking, while Nvidia contributes its Vera CPU, ConnectX NICs, Bluefield DPUs, NVLink interconnect, Spectrum-X switches, and rack-scale AI compute capabilities.

This partnership extends well beyond a typical supplier arrangement and represents a significant validation of Marvell's role in the artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure stack. The collaboration also encompasses silicon photonics technology and plans to transform telecommunications networks into AI infrastructure through Nvidia's Aerial AI-RAN for 5G and 6G applications. Marvell's recent $3.25 billion acquisition of Celestial AI, which brings photonic fabric technology for faster chip-to-chip data movement, directly complements this Nvidia partnership and positions the company at a critical bottleneck in GPU interconnect architecture.

More News from Barchart

Trump Says Micron Is One of the 'Hottest' Stocks. Does That Make MU a Buy Here?

Stocks Set to Open Higher as Bond Yields Fall on Fading Rate-Hike Bets, U.S. Jobs Data and Powell’s Remarks Awaited

Micron Stock Cools Off — Is MU Now Too Cheap to Ignore?

Stop Missing Market Moves: Get the FREE Barchart Brief – your midday dose of stock movers, trending sectors, and actionable trade ideas, delivered right to your inbox. Sign Up Now!

Should You Buy Marvell Stock, Too?

From a fundamental standpoint, Marvell delivered record fiscal 2026 revenue of $8.195 billion, representing 42% year-over-year growth, with earnings per share (EPS) climbing 81%. Management has guided fiscal 2027 revenue above $11 billion, with data center revenue expected to grow roughly 40% year-over-year and sequential acceleration in each quarter. Design wins and bookings are at all-time highs, and Q1 fiscal 2027 revenue guidance of $2.4 billion at the midpoint signals strong forward visibility.

The stock is now trading above its 20-day, 50-day, and 100-day moving averages, bringing its year-to-date gain north of 11%.

However, investors should weigh several important considerations before buying at these elevated levels. The Nvidia investment is part of the company’s broader pattern of $2 billion capital deployments across the AI supply chain, with prior recipients including Lumentum (LITE), Coherent (COHR), Synopsys (SNPS), CoreWeave (CRWV), and Nebius Group (NBIS).

Story Continues

Some analysts, including those at Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, have flagged concerns about increasing circularity in the AI ecosystem – where a small group of chip companies, cloud providers, and AI labs finance one another's buildouts, potentially inflating demand and valuations without generating proportionate new economic value.

MRVL Looks Expensive After the Nvidia Deal

Marvell's valuation also warrants scrutiny. Prior to the Nvidia announcement, a discounted cash flow analysis placed the stock's intrinsic value at roughly $83.30 per share, suggesting shares were already slightly above fair value before today’s rally.

The trailing price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio stood at 42, and the PEG ratio near 0.89, which is fairly priced for a high-growth semiconductor name, but hardly a deep discount. The post-announcement price implies investors are now paying a meaningful premium for the Nvidia partnership's strategic optionality.

A Tricky Macro Backdrop for Investors

The macro backdrop adds further complexity. The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) is closing out its worst quarter since 2022, driven by inflation fears from surging oil prices related to the Iran conflict, private credit market stress, and a broad pullback in technology stocks. Morgan Stanley's cross-asset team has recommended shifting to a more defensive posture, increasing cash and Treasury allocations.

Semiconductor stocks broadly have been breaking to new multi-month lows, and the geopolitical environment poses risks to AI infrastructure buildout timelines through higher energy and construction costs. Additionally, analysts now warn of threats to the helium supply sparked by the Iran war, which could create disruptions for the semiconductor industry.

What’s the Bottom Line on MRVL Stock Now?

On balance, the Nvidia investment is a powerful endorsement of Marvell's technology portfolio and its centrality to the AI data center scaling challenge. The combination of custom silicon, Ethernet networking, optical DSP, and silicon photonics gives Marvell differentiated positioning against competitors like Broadcom (AVGO), though Broadcom retains more than 70% market share in custom AI accelerators.

For investors with a longer time horizon and tolerance for volatility, Marvell's accelerating revenue trajectory, deepened Nvidia integration, and expanding design win pipeline create a compelling growth narrative, but the significant single-day price jump, elevated broader market risks, and circularity concerns in AI spending suggest that waiting for a pullback rather than chasing this surge would be the more prudent approach.

news.Title: AMAT Deepens its AI-Centric Product Expertise: Will it Deliver Growth?
news.Content:
Applied Materials AMAT is gaining from the high demand for its wafer equipment manufacturing products, driven by the rising use of advanced chips by AI data centers and hyperscalers. AMAT provides solutions across multiple fabrication steps, like deposition, materials engineering, etch, metrology and packaging, making it crucial in the chip space.

Applied Materials and SK hynix have formed a long-term R&D partnership to accelerate next-generation memory innovation, particularly in DRAM and high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for AI applications. The collaboration will focus on new materials, advanced process technologies and 3D packaging to enhance performance and efficiency.

Applied Materials and Micron Technology have formed a strategic partnership to advance next-generation AI memory solutions in the United States. The collaboration will focus on developing advanced DRAM, HBM and NAND technologies, combining Applied Materials’ process expertise with Micron’s manufacturing capabilities.

AMAT’s DRAM offerings are gaining traction as customers are aggressively investing in 6F² nodes supported by rising demand for high bandwidth memory DRAM, driven by AI workloads. On its first-quarter 2026 earnings call, AMAT highlighted its record growth in both Logic and DRAM segments, driven by major semiconductor transitions.

AMAT expects its leading-edge foundry, logic, DRAM and HBM to be the fastest-growing wafer fabrication equipment businesses in 2026. AMAT also gains from the increasing complexity and size of HBM chips, making it highly equipment-intensive. This is good for AMAT as the market for HBM expands. The company is determined to reach $3 billion in the next few years.

How Competitors Fare Against AMAT

Lam Research LRCX secured multiple critical etch wins at a major DRAM manufacturer with its new Akara etch system, which supports 3D DRAM architectures. This was supported by LRCX’s customer investments in DDR5, LPDDR5 and high-bandwidth memory. Additionally, Lam Research’s Aether dry-resist technology was selected as the production tool of record for a leading DRAM customer, securing a foothold in this high-growth segment.

ASML Holding ASML is experiencing strong demand from DRAM and logic customers, which are ramping leading-edge nodes using ASML’s NXE:3800E EUV systems. Additionally, ASML noted that multiple DRAM customers are adopting EUV lithography, which helps in shortening cycle time and lowering costs. However, AMAT offers a broad range of WFE products that do not compete directly with ASML and Lam Research, making it a stock worth holding.

Story Continues

AMAT’s Price Performance, Valuation and Estimates

Shares of Applied Materials have surged 48.4% in the past six months against the Zacks Electronics - Semiconductors industry’s decline of 2.4%.

AMAT Six-Month Performance Chart

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

From a valuation standpoint, Applied Materials trades at a forward price-to-sales ratio of 7.64X, higher than the industry’s average of 6.71X.

AMAT Forward 12-Month (P/S) Valuation Chart

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for Applied Materials’ fiscal 2026 and 2027 earnings implies year-over-year growth of 17.9% and 26.4%, respectively. The estimates for fiscal 2026 and 2027 have been revised upward over the past 60 and 30 days, respectively.

Applied Materials currently carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Title: 快訊》川普擬停戰 道指飆600點 台積電ADR強彈3％
news.Content:
（23：30更新）美國總統川普的最新表態再度讓市場上演逆轉行情。他在自家社群平台Truth Social上發文，宣稱伊朗已被「基本摧毀」，認為最困難的軍事階段已經結束，加上據《華爾街日報》報導，川普已告訴幕僚，即便仍未讓荷姆茲海峽重新開放，他也願意結束戰爭，相關言論都被視為停戰訊號。受衝突或將結束的樂觀情緒推動，周二（31日）美股一開盤，主要指數同步走高。道瓊工業指數早盤一度飆漲逾600點，那斯達克指數大漲逾400點。與台股連動密切的費城半導體指數盤中大漲逾3%，而台積電ADR也一改前一個交易日的沉重跌勢，早盤一度強彈逾3%。

截至台北時間31日晚間11點30分，道瓊工業指數上漲457.83點（或1.01%），暫報45673.97點；那斯達克指數上漲376.534點（或1.81%），暫報21171.175點；標普500指數上漲85.09點（或1.34%），暫報6428.81點；費城半導體指數上漲196.60點（或2.75%），暫報7338.93點。

5大科技巨頭同步走高，蘋果漲0.55%，谷歌母公司Alphabet漲2.84%，微軟漲1.42%，亞馬遜漲2.42%，臉書母公司Meta漲3.64%。半導體類股同樣漲勢強勁，輝達漲3.11%，超微漲1.34%，台積電ADR漲2.98%，聯電ADR漲2.73%。

儘管伊朗持續襲擊船隻與波斯灣國家基礎設施，川普仍堅稱荷姆茲海峽「不存在真正威脅」。KCM Trade首席市場分析師Tim Waterer 表示，「短期來看，戰爭結束無疑是件好事。但如果荷姆茲海峽繼續關閉，全球能源市場將面臨進一步的供應中斷風險。」

荷蘭資管公司Robeco多元資產策略主管Colin Graham指出，股市是在按照美國政府說的話來交易，也就是他們將結束這場戰爭，然而「市場還沒有進入第二層思考，即荷姆茲海峽仍有可能繼續關閉」

VanEck Associates Corp.跨資產策略師Anna Wu認為，風險資產一直在等待任何反彈契機，「因此市場情緒的轉變就像腎上腺素一樣刺激著市場。然而，目前這還不是定論，也並非最終結果。現在就將其作為基本預期，可能還為時過早。」

• 台積電ADR一改前一日跌勢，開盤上漲逾2%。
• 分析師認為，短期戰爭結束對股市是好事，但若荷姆茲海峽持續關閉，全球能源市場將面臨供應中斷風險。

news.Title: Unlocking Intrinsic Value: Analysis of Meta Platforms Inc
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . In this article, we will take a look into Meta Platforms Inc's ( NASDAQ:META ) DCF analysis, a reliable and data-driven approach to estimating its intrinsic value. Instead of using future free cash flow as in the traditional DCF model, the GuruFocus DCF calculator uses EPS without NRI as the default for the DCF model based on research that shows that historically stock prices have been more correlated with earnings than free cash ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms Inc.'s intrinsic value, calculated by GuruFocus's DCF model, is estimated at $730.56, suggesting it is modestly undervalued at its current trading price of $536.38.
• The DCF model uses EPS without NRI and a two-stage growth approach, with a 10-year growth stage at 22.70% and a terminal stage at 4%.
• A traditional DCF model based on free cash flow estimates the intrinsic value at $560.10, indicating fair valuation with a 4.23% margin of safety.
• The model's accuracy depends on various assumptions and projections about future earnings potential and growth rates.
news.Keywords: DCF Analysis, Intrinsic Value, Valuation Model, Margin of Safety, Financial Projections

news.Title: Wild Charts Show Pain Points in S&P 500’s Worst Month Since 2022
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Tech megacaps entered a correction, oil prices broke out, big-money funds retreated and small-lot investors showed waning conviction in buying the dip. While the war in Iran that triggered all that didn’t end a three-year bull run in US equities, it is shaking it to its core. With one day to go in March, the S&P 500 Index is on track for the worst month and worst quarter since 2022. Technology stocks were hit particularly hard, owing to a double-whammy of geopolitical angst and worries over ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The S&P 500 is on track for its worst month and quarter since 2022, with tech megacaps hit hard by geopolitical angst and AI disruption worries.
• Microsoft (MSFT) has fallen 26% this year amid uncertainty over capital spending and competition from AI startups.
• The Nasdaq 100 is in correction territory, trading at 21 times projected 12-month profits, a narrow gap with the S&P 500.
• Energy stocks have gained 39% year-to-date, benefiting from soaring oil prices due to the Iran war.
news.Keywords: Market Correction, Tech Sector Decline, Geopolitical Impact, Energy Stocks, AI Disruption

A general sell-off in artificial intelligence (AI) stocks impacted the company amid massive capital expenditures (capex) spending. Investors also harbor doubts about its partnership with OpenAI. However, amid these conditions, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft (MSFT) stock has fallen 33.5% from its 52-week high, impacted by AI stock sell-offs and massive capital expenditures.
• Despite concerns about OpenAI's financial stability, Microsoft has a $344 billion backlog excluding OpenAI's commitments.

news.Title: Trump’s Tax Cut Delivers at Least $65 Billion Windfall to Corporations
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Some of the country’s wealthiest corporations are calculating they owe far less to the Internal Revenue Service as a result of President Donald Trump’s overhauled tax code, underscoring how a law billed as a middle‑class cut also turned out to be a big win for Corporate America. Nearly a dozen of the 50 biggest US-listed companies attributed a drop in federal cash income taxes last year as a direct result of Trump’s $3.4 trillion sweeping tax law, according to a Bloomberg analysis of regulatory filings. In all, annual corporate tax revenues dropped by $65 billion following the law’s ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon.com Inc. saw a significant drop in federal cash income taxes in 2025, owing $2.8 billion down from over $7 billion in prior years.
• The company attributes this decrease to Trump's tax law, which it expects to continue reducing cash taxes in 2026.
• The tax law provided benefits across various sectors, including Big Tech, retailers, and manufacturers, through deductions and special preferences.
• Corporate tax revenues dropped by $65 billion annually following the law's passage.
news.Keywords: Tax Law Impact, Corporate Taxes, Tax Reduction, Regulatory Filings, Fiscal Policy

news.Title: This Unstoppable Vanguard ETF Is Beating the S&P 500, the Nasdaq-100, and the Dow Jones in 2026
news.Content: The U.S. stock market is off to a difficult start to 2026. Ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have sent the price of oil soaring, which is creating economic instability and clouding the earnings picture for corporate America. As a result, the S&P 500 , the Nasdaq-100 , and the Dow Jones Industrial Average have each suffered year-to-date declines of more than 5% so far. However, another index is bucking the losses to trade flat for the year. The Russell 2000 is home to approximately 2,000 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF (VTWO) is trading flat for the year, outperforming the S&P 500, Nasdaq-100, and Dow Jones.
• Small-cap stocks in the Russell 2000 are more insulated from global conflicts due to their domestic focus.
• The ETF's diversification and low weighting of top holdings (5.6%) contribute to steadier returns compared to concentrated indexes.
• Companies like Bloom Energy and Credo Technology within the ETF have shown significant stock price appreciation due to strong demand in their respective sectors.
news.Keywords: Small-Cap Stocks, ETF Performance, Diversification, Market Insulation, Sector Demand

news.Title: This Is the Cheapest "Magnificent Seven" Stock Right Now. Is it a Value Play or Value Trap?
news.Content: The once seemingly invincible "Magnificent Seven" stocks have not fared well this year. The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF has fallen by more than 9%, underperforming the broader market. Investors may see this as a buying opportunity, given that most still see immense potential with artificial intelligence (AI). But one should never buy a stock or an exchange-traded fund (ETF) simply because it is down. Valuation is also important. This is the cheapest Magnificent Seven stock right now. Is it a value play or a value trap? Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms (META) has the lowest valuation among the Magnificent Seven, trading at less than 20 times forward earnings.
• Meta projects significant capex in 2026 ($115-$135 billion) to support AI infrastructure, including third-party cloud providers and data centers.
• Investors are concerned about Zuckerberg's tendency to make large, potentially undisciplined bets on new technologies.
• Meta's ad revenue grew 24% YoY in 2025, with AI expected to enhance ad targeting and engagement.
news.Keywords: Magnificent Seven, Valuation Analysis, AI Capex, Advertising Revenue, Investment Discipline

news.Title: Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights Adobe's and Microsoft
news.Content: For Immediate Release Chicago, IL – March 31, 2026 – Today, Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights Adobe’s ADBE and Microsoft MSFT. 2 Great Tech Stocks to Buy Now, Down -65% and -35% Artificial intelligence expansion and innovations rocked software stocks in the first quarter of 2026. Wall Street is worried about rapid AI advancements and their long-term impacts on established software giants such as Adobe. Meanwhile, investors have also, perhaps to less fanfare, sold off some of the AI hyperscalers to start 2026 as they worry their massive AI capex spending plans aren’t paying off quickly enough. Microsoft, for instance, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Adobe (ADBE) and Microsoft (MSFT) are trading at their lowest forward earnings multiples in years, presenting potential buying opportunities.

• Microsoft's stock is down 35% from highs due to AI spending and OpenAI partnership concerns, but Azure revenue grew 39% YoY.

• 原因是台灣去年已量產2奈米，熊本廠3奈米預計2028年投產，不違反最先進製程留台原則。

• 此為台積電歷年最大單筆匯出賺息避險投資，約9600億台幣。

news.Title: Got $3,000? 2 Cloud Stocks That Wall Street Analysts Raised Targets on This Month.
news.Content: Cloud computing stocks have been an interesting case this year. Although demand for cloud computing continues to surge, many of the stocks in this industry have sputtered. One of the reasons is the stocks have become too expensive and overvalued after three years of bull market gains. But there are other reasons specific to cloud stocks. Many are dealing with capacity constraints to handle the high demand, which has hurt their ability to maximize profits. Will AI create the world's first ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings (ARM) is launching its own CPU chip, the Arm AGI CPU, for data centers handling agentic AI workloads, with Meta Platforms as a key customer.
• Arm projects the new chips to generate $15 billion annually starting in 2031, significantly increasing total revenue and earnings.
• Needham upgraded Arm to a Buy with a $200 price target, citing its high-stakes bets and transformation for the better.
• Despite a high forward P/E ratio of 61, Arm is seen as a credible AI play due to the rise of agentic AI and the growing role of CPUs in AI data centers.
news.Keywords: CPU Chip, Agentic AI, Data Center Market, Semiconductor Design, Growth Potential

(2408)南亞科-公告本公司取得機器設備

1.標的物之名稱及性質（如坐落台中市北區ＸＸ段ＸＸ小段土地）:

機器設備

2.事實發生日:114/12/24~115/3/31

3.董事會通過日期: 不 ...

• 南亞科公告取得機器設備一批，交易總金額約新台幣11.47億元，供生產用途。
• 交易相對人為日本設備商MURATA MACHINERY, LTD.。
• 南亞科規劃2026年資本支出將大幅攀升至500億元，年增273.13%，主要用於新廠建置。

news.Title: York Space and Firefly Are Riding SpaceX's Coattails. Smaller Space Stocks Post Strong Gains.
news.Content: Last Wednesday was a great day to be a space investor. (Thursday and Friday, somewhat less so!) Rumors that SpaceX would file its IPO prospectus , which turned out to be false, set space stocks on fire. Shares of recent space IPOs York Space Systems (NYSE: YSS) and Firefly Aerospace (NASDAQ: FLY) shot up ...
news.AI_Summary:
• York Space Systems and Firefly Aerospace saw significant stock gains on rumors of SpaceX's IPO filing, but gave back gains afterward.
• SpaceX is rumored to be a $16 billion/year company with about $3 billion in annual profit, significantly larger than York and Firefly.
• York Space Systems has a market cap of $2.7 billion but is losing $84.5 million per year and burning $130 million in negative free cash flow.
• The potential SpaceX IPO could pull investors away from smaller space stocks like York and Firefly.
news.Keywords: Space IPO Rumors, Market Capitalization, Cash Burn, Free Cash Flow, Investor Sentiment

news.Title: 外媒點台積電有望衝「3兆美元俱樂部」！揭沒怕2關鍵防火：已準備好
news.Content:
財經中心／綜合報導

美國投資媒體點名台積電（TSMC）有望躋身「3兆美元俱樂部」，與輝達（Nvidia）、蘋果（Apple）、Alphabet等科技 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 外媒點名台積電有望躋身「3兆美元俱樂部」，與輝達、蘋果、Alphabet等科技巨頭並列。
• 分析認為，台積電掌握AI與先進製程兩大關鍵戰力，是市場看好其長期成長的重要原因。
• 台積電身為全球最大晶圓代工廠，在先進製程領域具領先優勢，掌握約71%晶圓代工市占。
• 高效能運算、AI與資料中心相關晶片已占台積電營收約55%。
news.Keywords: 台積電,3兆美元俱樂部,AI,先進製程,晶圓代工

news.Title: Why 1 Top Analyst Says Apple Is a Screaming Buy Right Now
news.Content: Tech is a wreck. So far in 2026, the technology sector is the second-worst-performing sector in the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) . Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) , which ranks as the world's second-largest technology company by market cap , is among the laggards. Shares of the iPhone maker are down more than 10% year to date. However, Wall Street remains generally bullish about Apple. The consensus ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring believes Apple (AAPL) is a 'screaming buy,' with a price target suggesting nearly 28% upside.
• A survey indicates a record high number of consumers in the U.S. and China plan to upgrade to new iPhones.
• Apple is expected to be the only major global smartphone vendor gaining market share in 2026.
• Demand for foldable iPhones appears robust, with potential for significant revenue contribution.
news.Keywords: iPhone Upgrades, Market Share Gain, Foldable Phones, Consumer Demand, Analyst Optimism

news.Title: The Nasdaq Is Officially in a Correction. Here's What Investors Should Do, According to History.
news.Content: The Nasdaq-100 index tracks the performance of the top 100 companies (by value) listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange, excluding banks and other financial institutions. A whopping 60% of the index's portfolio is parked in the technology sector alone, so it has a high degree of exposure to rapidly growing artificial intelligence (AI) companies like Nvidia . As a result, the Nasdaq-100 often outperforms more diversified indexes like the S&P 500 , but it also experiences more volatility during periods of market turmoil. The index is currently in correction territory after declining by more than ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Nasdaq-100 index is in correction territory, down over 10% from its peak, due to spiking oil prices and AI disruption worries.
• Nvidia, the largest holding in the Nasdaq-100 (8.5% weighting), is impacted by broader market downturns.
• Concerns exist about a potential slowdown in AI data center infrastructure spending, particularly regarding OpenAI's commitments.
• History suggests that market corrections can present buying opportunities for patient investors.
news.Keywords: Market Correction, Nasdaq-100, AI Spending, Geopolitical Impact, Investment Opportunity

news.Title: Amazon Stock Investors Just Got Great News Concerning OpenAI and Robotaxis
news.Content: Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) shares have fallen 20% from their high due in part to concerns about the company's plan to spend about $200 billion on capital expenditures this year. Also, recession fears have resurfaced because the U.S.-Iran war has pushed oil prices to a multiyear high. That has hurt consumer discretionary stocks like Amazon. However, Amazon shareholders recently got some great news about the company's artificial intelligence and robotaxi businesses. Here are the important details. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon Web Services (AWS) and OpenAI expanded their partnership, with Amazon investing $50 billion and OpenAI committing $138 billion in AWS cloud services over eight years.
• OpenAI will consume about 2 gigawatts of Trainium capacity through AWS, adding credibility to Amazon's custom AI chips.
• AWS will offer a stateful runtime environment (SRE) powered by OpenAI models on Amazon Bedrock, enabling deeper integration than Microsoft Azure's stateless APIs.
• AWS reported 24% revenue growth in Q4, its fastest in over four years, and this partnership could accelerate that growth.
news.Keywords: Cloud Partnership, AI Investment, Custom AI Chips, Stateful Runtime, Revenue Acceleration

news.Title: Is Nvidia a Smart Buy for a Value Investor Right Now?
news.Content: The hottest tech stocks of the times generally aren't on the buy list of value investors -- and this is because they often trade at lofty valuations. Excitement about the prospects of these players prompts investors seeking growth to rush into these names. We've seen this over the past few years with technology stocks focused on artificial intelligence (AI). Companies from Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) to Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) saw their stock prices climb in the triple and quadruple ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is considered a smart buy for value investors, trading at its lowest level in about a year.
• The company has reported explosive revenue gains (double and triple digits) due to its leading position in AI chips (GPUs).
• Nvidia's GPUs are the fastest, allowing customers to bring AI platforms to market sooner.
• Analysts expect annual revenue to climb 72% in the current full year, making the stock appear undervalued.
news.Keywords: Value Investing, AI Chips, GPU Market, Revenue Growth, Undervalued Stock

news.Title: Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller Piled Into 2 of the Hottest AI Stocks for a Second Consecutive Quarter (No, Not Nvidia or Palantir)
news.Content: Data is the fuel that keeps Wall Street's engine turning. Unfortunately for investors, the amount of data they need to digest can be overwhelming. Between earnings reports and economic data releases, it's easy for something to slip through the cracks. For example, Feb. 17 marked the deadline for institutional investors with at least ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller has increased his stakes in Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) for the second consecutive quarter.
• Druckenmiller favors Alphabet and Amazon over Nvidia and Palantir, seeing AI application stocks as the future.
• Alphabet's Google Cloud reported 48% revenue growth, while Amazon Web Services saw sales growth reaccelerate to 24% YoY.
• Both companies are seen as historically inexpensive relative to their forecasted cash flow in 2027.
news.Keywords: Billionaire Investment, AI Stocks, Cloud Growth, Valuation Analysis, Tech Giants

news.Title: 2 Beaten-Down AI Stocks to Buy Before the Next Bull Market
news.Content: Although it's still officially in a bull market overall, the artificial intelligence (AI) investment sector may have something to say about that claim. Many AI stocks are down more than 20% from their all-time highs, knocking them out of bull market territory on an individual stock basis. However, once geopolitical tensions stabilize and investors realize that AI spending will last for many more years, some of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft (MSFT) stock is down over 30% from its highs, presenting a contrarian buy opportunity.
• Azure's revenue rose 39% year over year in Q2 FY26, contributing to Microsoft's overall 17% revenue growth.
• Concerns about AI agents rewriting software are seen as unfounded for Microsoft's business-centric products.
• Broadcom (AVGO) is expected to see monster growth from its custom AI chip business, projecting $100 billion or greater revenue by the end of next year.
news.Keywords: Contrarian Buy, AI Stocks, Cloud Computing, Custom AI Chips, Growth Potential

news.Title: 《韓股》韓股重挫逾4% 韓元創17年新低
news.Content: 【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】受到外資大舉賣超3.8兆韓元的衝擊，韓國股市31日大幅下跌224.84點或4 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 受外資大舉賣超3.8兆韓元的衝擊，韓國股市31日大幅下跌224.84點或4.26%，收在5052.46點。
• 韓元匯價走貶，創17年來最低，兌一美元在1,530.1價位附近。
• 分析師表示，伊朗危機加上油價上漲，推高股票的風險溢酬，韓企獲利預估可能下修。
• 個股中，三星電子大跌5.16%，SK海力士重挫7.56%。
news.Keywords: 韓股,外資賣超,韓元,中東衝突,記憶體

news.Title: 攻邊緣AI商機，磐儀擬辦理上限2千萬股私募案，引進策略投資人
news.Content: 【財訊快報／記者王宜弘報導】IPC廠磐儀(3594)搶攻邊緣AI商機，週二(31日)董事會決議辦理私募 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• IPC廠磐儀(3594)為搶攻邊緣AI商機，董事會決議辦理私募現金增資，引進策略投資人，私募股數不超過2千萬股。
• 磐儀去年稅後盈餘4666萬元，每股盈餘0.49元，獲利下滑主因是美國對等關稅及新台幣匯率波動。
• 磐儀董事會決議配發0.4元現金股利，發放率81%，殖利率逾1%。
• 磐儀與聯發科(2454)合資成立磐旭智能，劍指工業手持物聯網裝置市場，今年營運可望爆發。
news.Keywords: 磐儀,邊緣AI,私募,聯發科,營運

news.Content: 【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】31日，日本日經225指數開低走低，盤中雖有短暫翻紅，但上漲力道不足走勢疲弱，最後收在51,063.72點 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 受中東衝突持續影響，日股31日開低走低，日經225指數收跌822.13點或1.58%，月線更是大跌超過13%。
• 分析認為，美國半導體相關類股大幅下挫，拖累日本股市出現拋售壓力。
• 半導體族群一片綠油油，愛德萬測試大跌2.8%，東京威力科創大跌4.2%。
• AI概念股中，軟銀重挫3.7%，藤倉暴跌超過9%。

• 初期活動將涵蓋馬祖場（4月16-19日）及屏東場（5月21-23日）。

news.Title: The Best Quantum Computing Stock to Buy With $1,000 Right Now
news.Content: While the world fixates on Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) chips fueling today's artificial intelligence (AI) boom, the company is quietly positioning itself as a foundational player in quantum computing. With a modest sum of $1,000 to invest, Nvidia stands out as the smartest quantum AI play -- not because it builds the hardware, but because ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia is positioning itself as a foundational player in quantum computing by developing software that bridges classical supercomputers and quantum systems.
• The company's CUDA software platform is being extended into quantum toolkits, allowing simulations on Nvidia GPUs that would take years on ordinary computers.
• Nvidia's ecosystem makes quantum experimentation scalable and affordable, offering investors exposure to quantum AI progress beyond raw chip sales.
• The company's data center business generates billions in profit, funding its quantum roadmap, and its ecosystem creates a unique flywheel advantage.
• Investors may not be pricing in ancillary AI-driven growth from quantum simulation royalties, hybrid cloud integrations, and enterprise software licensing.
news.Keywords: quantum computing, AI, software platform, CUDA, hybrid computing

news.Title: Beyond Nvidia: This Under-the-Radar AI Stock Is Up Nearly 300% Over the Past Year
news.Content: Nvidia has been a great investment over the past few years, and I still think it's a great stock pick now. However, many investors are looking for alternatives or new ways to invest in the artificial intelligence (AI) space, and I think I've identified ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron (MU) stock has seen significant gains, up nearly 300% over the past year, and is considered a strong investment in the AI space.
• Micron is a critical supplier of High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI computing chips, with the HBM market expected to grow from $35 billion in 2025 to $100 billion by 2028.
• The company currently cannot meet total HBM demand, with capacity only meeting half to two-thirds of demand until 2027.
• Despite Google's TurboQuant algorithm reducing memory demand, it only affects one part of large language models, and overall memory demand is expected to remain strong.
• Micron expects significant revenue growth, projecting $33.5 billion next quarter, up from $23.9 billion this quarter.
news.Keywords: AI, memory, HBM, demand, growth trajectory

news.Title: 聯發科推動文化永續傳統，首度藝企合作發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」
news.Content: 【財訊快報／記者李純君報導】IC設計龍頭聯發科(2454)今日宣布首度藝企合作，與專精台灣傳統音樂的灣聲樂團，共同發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• IC設計龍頭聯發科(2454)宣布首度藝企合作，與灣聲樂團共同發起「臺灣共鳴－公益音樂行腳」計畫。
• 計畫將融合臺灣文化靈魂的古典樂，帶入全臺校園、社區與公共領域，提升大眾文化近用性。
• 聯發科董事長蔡明介表示，將影響力擴大至在地人文的文化永續推廣。
• 灣聲樂團成立於2017年，是臺灣唯一以演出「臺灣音樂」為核心訴求的職業樂團。
news.Keywords: 聯發科,灣聲樂團,公益音樂,文化推廣,ESG

news.Title: 《台北股市》台股居3萬點高檔逾千檔股價破年線 QIC提四策略助力企業
news.Content: 【時報記者張佳琪台北報導】台股穩居3萬點之上，日成交金額8000億元幾乎成為常態，使得台股已晉升全球前十大的關鍵資本市場。不過，仍有1071家、高達50%的公司股價跌破年線，寬量國際(QIC)創辦人暨執行長李鴻基今(31)日對 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股穩居3萬點之上，日成交金額8000億元，但仍有1071家公司股價跌破年線，比重高達50%。
• 寬量國際(QIC)認為，這些公司市值落後的主因在於「思維」問題，需換腦思考進入AI主導的新資本世代。
• QIC提出「四大行動綱領」：找回成長道路、論述長期願景、定義關鍵資訊、強化ESG行動。
• 建議企業應以營收或獲利成長付出溢價，而非僅高殖利率、價值投資。
news.Keywords: 台股,市值,年線,AI世代,資本市場

• POCO X8 Pro Max搭載8500mAh矽碳電池，POCO X8 Pro配置6500mAh電池，全系列支援100W快充。
• POCO X8 Pro Max搭載聯發科天璣9500s，跑分約308萬；POCO X8 Pro搭載天璣8500-Ultra，跑分逾228萬。
• 售價方面，POCO X8 Pro Max定價14,999元起，POCO X8 Pro為10,999元起，全系列將於4月1日開賣。

news.Content: Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN ) is one of the 10 best stocks that recently issued new debt . On March 25, Citi increased its price target on Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) from $265 to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Citi raised its price target for Amazon.com (AMZN) to $285 from $265, maintaining a Buy rating.
• Estimates for Amazon Web Services (AWS) were raised, driven by AI demand, with predictions of 28% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 and 37% in 2027.
• Wells Fargo maintained an Overweight rating with a $304 price target, highlighting compute capacity as a key success factor for AI-driven demand.
• Hyperscaler capacity is expected to double to 98GW, with annual CapEx reaching $860 billion by 2027, indicating significant cloud industry expansion.
• While Amazon is a strong investment, other AI stocks may offer greater upside potential and less downside risk.

news.Title: Is the AI Bubble Bursting? Here Are 2 Top Stocks to Buy the Dip on When It Does.
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) companies have driven the stock market higher in the past few years. Now, they may be contributing to it returning to Earth. Rapidly growing AI entities like OpenAI are scaling back commitments to chip spending and canceling projects such as video generator Sora, which could reduce overall chip spending. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The AI bubble may be bursting, with companies like OpenAI scaling back chip spending, potentially leading to a stock market bear market.
• This downturn presents a buying opportunity for long-term investors to acquire stocks at discounted prices.
• Airbnb (ABNB) is mentioned as a potential dip-buy, with consistent market share gains and 11% revenue growth last quarter.
• Interactive Brokers (IBKR) is also highlighted as a potential dip-buy, with 4.65 million active accounts and 31% year-over-year growth.
• Both ABNB and IBKR are seen as potentially attractive at lower prices if the AI bubble pops.
news.Keywords: AI bubble, bear market, dip-buying, tech stocks, market downturn

• 統計第一季跌幅前20大上市股票，永冠-KY（1589）因公司債違約、裁員等因素，股價重挫逾6成，成為本季跌幅最重個股。
• 生技股康霈*（6919）跌幅達44%，北極星藥業-KY（6550）跌幅38%。
• 華冠投顧分析師陳韋廷表示，康霈*股價趨勢已向下彎，整體趨勢已改變，不建議操作。
• 多為跌幅超過兩成的小型股，其中康霈*列入0050指數後市場出現雜音。
news.Keywords: 上市股票,跌幅,永冠-KY,康霈*,生技股

news.Title: 【台股Q1風雲榜】雍智科技狂飆229%最猛…中探針、青雲也翻倍漲！20檔上櫃飆股一表看
news.Content: 台股今（31）日迎來3月最後一個交易日，《Yahoo股市》統計今年第一季上櫃前20名飆股，幾乎清一色由電子族群包辦，由雍智科技（6683）以漲幅229%拔得頭籌，中探針（6217）以及青雲 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 統計第一季上櫃前20名飆股，電子股占比超過七成，由雍智科技（6683）以漲幅229%拔得頭籌。
• 中探針（6217）及青雲（5386）分別大漲219%、186%。
• 專家白易弘表示，這波行情多由AI題材推動，建議等待第二季若出現回檔修正時再進場。
• 光通訊族群聯亞、波若威等，建議等待第二季回檔修正、股價回到季線附近時再進場。
news.Keywords: 上櫃股,飆股,AI題材,雍智科技,光通訊

news.Title: 【盤後焦點】台股下跌795點創史上第12大跌點　3月重挫3691.5點
news.Content: （中央社記者曾仁凱台北2026年3月31日電）中東戰事與能源市場動盪，引發投資人擔憂，台積電今天早盤一度翻紅撐盤，午盤後賣壓湧現，台股盤中最多下殺812點，終場下跌79 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 中東戰事與能源市場動盪，引發投資人擔憂，台積電今天早盤一度翻紅撐盤，午盤後賣壓湧現，台股盤中最多下殺812點，終場下跌795.17點，為台股第12大收盤跌點。
• 指數跌破季線32181點，失守32000點關卡，累計3月重挫3691.5點。
• 三大權值股方面，台積電 (2330) 終場下跌20元或1.12%，收在全日最低點1760元。
• 國泰證期顧問處協理蔡明翰分析，台股現在是短空長多，AI基本面沒有太大改變，但短線受中東局勢干擾，牽動油價、通膨等，加上台股相對美股位階高太多，短線面臨修正壓力。
news.Keywords: 台股,下跌,台積電,季線,中東戰事

news.Content: 【財訊快報／方亞申】伊朗無別差攻擊越來越重，對於中東阿拉伯國家及伊朗發電、海水淡化等民生設施都不排除轟炸，刺激西德州原油價格再創106美元高點，美股原本週一有反彈契機，又被壓回，其中費半指數大跌4.23%，其他三大指數下跌不 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 伊朗攻擊中東阿拉伯國家民生設施，刺激西德州原油價格再創106美元高點，美股費半指數大跌4.23%，台股也跟著加大下跌。
• 終場指數重挫795.17點，收在31722.99點，成交量7924.5億元。
• 外資單月賣超近一兆、台幣貶破32關卡，操作上宜減量經營，等待利空鈍化。
• 指數季線正式跌破彈不上來，將退守31000點至3萬點關卡，拉長整理時間。
news.Keywords: 台股,下跌,原油價格,外資賣超,季線

news.Title: Agentic AI Could Be the Next Big Breakthrough -- and This Tech Giant May Already Be Ahead
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) is evolving fast. But the next phase may not just be about smarter models -- it could be about systems that actually take action. This new category of software, called agentic AI, is designed to take basic instructions about a task, then complete the steps to handle that task, make decisions, and operate with minimal additional human input. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Agentic AI, systems that can take action and operate with minimal human input, represents the next phase of AI evolution.
• The market for agentic AI is projected to grow from $5.2 billion in 2024 to $197 billion by 2034.
• Alibaba Group is actively building applied, agent-driven tools like its Wukong platform (an enterprise AI control center) and Accio Work (AI to run business operations).
• These products position Alibaba not just as an AI model builder, but as a provider of AI systems that can run real businesses.
• Alibaba's position as a leading cloud infrastructure provider in China offers a platform to deploy these tools at scale, though competition is intense.
news.Keywords: agentic AI, AI systems, automation, Alibaba, market growth

news.Title: 台積電家庭日改版　逾30萬人次參與創新高
news.Content: （中央社記者張建中新竹2026年3月31日電）台積電 (2330) 家庭日活動以環保減碳、多元彈性和地方創生 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台積電家庭日活動以環保減碳、多元彈性和地方創生為三大主軸，改以更貼近員工心聲的新形式舉行。
• 2025年累計有30萬人次參與，創下歷史新高，較前一年成長3倍。
• 家庭日將活動期間延長至7個月，場域由樂園擴展至文化園區、農場、動植物園等多元選擇。
• 提供每名員工3000點旅遊點數，可自由探索全台39個場域。
news.Keywords: 台積電,家庭日,ESG,員工福利,地方創生

news.Title: 《熱門族群》記憶體回檔 重演2025年DeepSeek誤判？
news.Content: 【時報記者葉時安台北報導】記憶體軍心渙散？隨Google的TurboQuant壓縮演算法發布後，且終端需求不振，近期記憶體現貨價格出現鬆動，尤其DRAM相關產品價格，記憶體大廠美光周一股價重挫9.8%，延續財報公布後的拋 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Google發表TurboQuant壓縮演算法，引發市場對記憶體需求前景疑慮，衝擊股價明顯拉回，但記憶體族群跌深反彈可期。
• 野村證券預料，記憶體多頭循環將走得比油價上漲更久。
• 南亞科私募引入Sandisk、鎧俠、Solidigm、Cisco等四「大咖」業者入股，可望確保長期穩定需求。
• 法人看好AI需求將支撐記憶體超級周期延續，企業級SSD及邊緣AI領域個股前景正向。
news.Keywords: 記憶體,Google,TurboQuant,南亞科,AI

news.Title: 南亞科等4檔跌停！美光崩3成爆記憶體大屠殺「劉德音防線」崩潰
news.Content: 美光財報後股價持續走弱，單日重挫近10%，收在321.8美元，自3月中旬公布財報以來，累計跌幅已達3成，不僅回吐前波漲幅，也 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美光財報後股價持續走弱，單日重挫近10%，累計跌幅已達3成，跌破前台積電董事長劉德音的成本區間。
• 市場解讀此波修正與Google推出新一代AI壓縮技術「TurboQuant」有關，擔憂未來AI對記憶體需求強度可能變化。
• 法人對產業長線仍持正面看法，認為技術演進不至於改變整體需求趨勢。
• 美光基本面仍維持強勁，客戶實際取得供貨量仍低於需求水準，高階記憶體產品如HBM4產能更已提前被預訂。
news.Keywords: 美光,記憶體,南亞科,跌停,AI

news.Title: 多頭總司令張錫：AI時代避開鬼故事擁抱基本面 跟著台積電走就對了
news.Content: 近期美、台股市同步陷入劇烈震盪，S&P 500 指數甚至跌破季線，引發投資人恐慌。對此，有「台股多頭總司令」之稱的國泰投信前董事長、東海大學財金系客座教授張錫於受訪時指出，台股憑藉強大的 AI 半導體供應鏈與內資資金 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 「台股多頭總司令」張錫指出，台股憑藉強大的AI半導體供應鏈與內資資金支撐，表現相對抗跌，指數回檔破季線反而是極佳買點。
• 他強調，台股已進入K型化時代，資金將更集中於具成長性的龍頭企業，建議避開跟不上AI的鬼故事公司。
• 張錫直言，「只要台積電持續成長，台股就不可能進入空頭。」
• 他認為，台灣企業應積極與資本市場溝通，利用上市籌資來壯大規模。
news.Keywords: 台股,多頭,台積電,AI,基本面

news.Title: 台股多頭總司令張錫：台積電1,800元以下閉眼買！明年上看3字頭、大盤4萬點要來了
news.Content: 美伊戰事不休，台股近日持續震盪，31日早盤最多下跌486點，跌破季線32,190點，台積電表現抗跌來到1,790元，素有「多頭總司 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 前國泰投信董事長張錫指出，台積電明年上看3,000元，預估EPS 125元，以25倍本益比來看，股價3字頭是合理的。
• 他認為，台股因具備AI剛性需求強勁、台灣位處AI重要供應鏈等基本面優勢，明年大盤有望站上4萬點。
• 張錫建議，台積電跌到1,800元以下可閉眼買，今年台股走勢與去年應會一樣，第一季修正、第四季再衝上來。
• 目前要擔心的是通膨，美國今年不至於會升息，但降息機會降低。
news.Keywords: 台積電,目標價,台股,AI,基本面

news.Title: 4月恐有大事發生？財經專家點名「他」更可怕
news.Content:
財經中心／李汶臻報導

2026年第一季進入尾聲，全球金融市場正屏息以待4月的到來。財訊傳媒董事長謝金河31日在臉書發文以「科技七雄的考驗時刻」為題指出，回顧美國總統川普上任以來，每年的3、4月經常有 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 財訊傳媒董事長謝金河點名，美國科技巨頭正遭遇劇烈調整，其中輝達(Nvidia)股價跌破年線，市值瀕臨跌破4兆美元關卡。
• 相較之下，台積電ADR、ASML、Intel等上游製造端是少數今年沒有下跌的個股。
• 謝金河認為，這代表半導體上游製造端仍有「天險可守」，在全球股市大調整浪潮中展現相對韌性。
• 他認為，4月可能會有大事發生，而輝達比特斯拉更可怕。

news.Title: Rivian and Lucid can operate like Tesla after new legislative win
news.Content: Tesla has always occupied a special place in the U.S. automotive landscape as one of the few original equipment manufacturers that does not have to adhere to state dealership franchise laws that have been in place for decades, but now rivals EV Rivian and Lucid are getting the same treatment in at least one state. The traditional dealership model forces consumers to visit showrooms where a third party acts as a middleman in the sale of the vehicle to them. "By bypassing the dealership, Tesla offers customers a more transparent buying experience with set prices and minimal negotiations," according to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Washington state has passed a bill allowing electric vehicle (EV) manufacturers Rivian and Lucid to sell vehicles directly to consumers, mirroring Tesla's exemption.
• Tesla has historically operated without state dealership franchise laws, offering a more transparent buying experience.
• Traditional dealerships have lobbied states to maintain franchise laws, impacting OEM sales models.
• About half of U.S. states have eased direct EV sales restrictions, while others maintain bans.
• This legislative win for Rivian and Lucid will increase competition for Tesla in direct-to-consumer sales in Washington state.
news.Keywords: EV sales, direct-to-consumer, dealership laws, Washington state, automotive

news.Title: Nvidia's Rubin Chip Arrives in Late 2026. Is Now the Time to Buy This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock?
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) is the poster child of the artificial intelligence (AI) industry. Its high-powered chips are the brains that power AI. The company's leading position in the chip industry isn't lost on Wall Street, with the stock up 50% over the past year and over 18,000% over the past decade. That said, Nvidia's stock is down nearly 20% from its 52-week high, and it has a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia's stock is down nearly 20% from its 52-week high, with a new chip platform, Rubin, expected in late 2026.
• The stock's P/E ratio is 34x, significantly lower than its five-year average of 64x, but still expensive compared to the broader tech sector.
• Rising oil and natural gas prices could increase electricity costs, making AI more expensive to use and build infrastructure for.
• Higher energy costs might also lead to a broader economic pullback, potentially pausing AI infrastructure spending.
• Investors are advised to keep Nvidia on their watchlist, as the AI bubble may continue to deflate, offering better entry points later.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, AI, valuation, energy prices, market correction

news.Title: Alphabet vs. Amazon: Both AI Stocks Have Been Hammered, but One Looks Like a Better Buy Now
news.Content: Tech stocks have been slammed in 2026, with recent trading days compounding the pain for investors. Even industry leaders like Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) and Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) have seen their shares take a hit. As of this writing, the two stocks are both down about 13% year to date. With both tech companies investing aggressively in artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, the recent ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Both Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) stocks are down about 13% year-to-date, presenting potential buying opportunities amid aggressive AI infrastructure investment.
• Amazon's AWS saw revenue growth of 24% year-over-year to $35.6 billion in Q4 2025, driven by AI workloads, with expected capital expenditures of $200 billion in 2026.
• Alphabet's Google Cloud revenue jumped 48% year-over-year to $17.7 billion in Q4 2025, with AI driving significant benefits across its ecosystem.
• Alphabet anticipates 2026 capital expenditures between $175 billion and $185 billion.
• Alphabet is considered the better buy due to its faster cloud growth (48% vs. 24%) and slightly cheaper valuation (P/E of ~25 vs. ~28 for Amazon).
news.Keywords: Alphabet, Amazon, AI, cloud growth, valuation

news.AI_Summary:

• The company's Rule of 40 score is 127%, reflecting strong revenue growth and adjusted operating margin.

news.Title: How Much Further Could Tesla Stock Fall?
news.Content: It has been a painful start to 2026 for Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) investors. With shares trading at about $356 as of this writing, the company has lost more than a fifth of its value since the start of the year. A decline of this magnitude might look like a tempting entry point. But is this pullback a genuine buying opportunity, or could shares of the electric-car maker fall even further? Ultimately, I think a strong case could be made for the stock going significantly lower. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's stock has fallen over a fifth year-to-date, trading at $356, with a potential for further decline.
• The company's operating margin contracted to 5.7% in Q4, and EPS plunged 60% year-over-year to $0.24, resulting in a P/E ratio of ~330.
• Full-year vehicle deliveries fell 9% and automotive revenue declined 10% year-over-year, with sequential deliveries expected to drop 13% in Q1.
• Heavy investment in autonomous driving, custom chips, and humanoid robots increases risk, with capital expenditures topping $8.5 billion in 2025.
• While FSD subscriptions and energy storage show growth, the core EV business faces pressure, and the stock's valuation is considered expensive.

news.Title: Everyone Is Rotating Out of Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks. Here's Why That Could Be a Costly Mistake in 2026.
news.Content: The S&P 500' s 7% year-to-date pullback has been largely led by artificial intelligence (AI) stocks suddenly being seen as liabilities rather than assets. When Microsoft, Alphabet , Oracle, and Amazon all recently ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI stocks, including Microsoft and Palantir, have seen significant pullbacks, with Microsoft down 26% and Palantir down nearly 20% year-to-date.
• This sell-off is attributed to steep valuations and concerns about the high cost of AI infrastructure investment.
• Despite the correction, AI solutions are still in high demand, and the industry's stocks may offer more upside than downside.
• Unlike dot-com stocks, current AI companies have real cash flows and better fundamentals, making them less speculative.

news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) shares closed down 9.88% as investors shifted focus from AI-driven results to concerns about Google's TurboQuant AI algorithm.
• TurboQuant could curb memory demand by compressing memory requirements by up to six times.
• Soaring memory demand was a key driver for Micron's 250%+ surge over the past year.
• The company's blockbuster earnings earlier this month have been followed by a downward trend as investors question growth sustainability and its $25 billion+ capex plan.
• The full impact of Google's algorithm is unclear, but more efficient models could potentially drive increased demand.
news.Keywords: Micron, AI, memory demand, TurboQuant, semiconductor

news.Title: Was Artificial Intelligence (AI) Just the Biggest Hype Cycle of This Generation? Here's What the Numbers Actually Say.
news.Content: Investors always look for the next big growth area, an innovation or trend that may transform business or our daily lives. That's because getting in on companies involved could produce mind-blowing returns. In recent years, investors identified artificial intelligence (AI) as the next major thing -- and they piled into shares of companies driving this technology. Names such as AI chip designer Nvidia , cloud player Alphabet , and networking expert Broadcom soared, leading the S&P 500 to record levels and significant gains. The benchmark advanced nearly 80% over the past three calendar years. Will AI create the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI stocks like Nvidia, Alphabet, and Broadcom have soared, but have recently lost momentum due to concerns about valuations, AI spending pace, and economic uncertainty.
• Evidence suggests AI-driven growth is meeting expectations, with AWS reaching a $142 billion annual revenue run rate.
• Palantir Technologies is seeing faster and larger contract expansions, with one utility company increasing its annual contract value from $7 million to $31 million.
• These examples indicate that AI is delivering results and meeting user expectations.
• Despite recent headwinds, the numbers suggest AI is not falling short of its initial promises.
news.Keywords: AI, hype cycle, growth, revenue, market momentum

news.Title: Micron Stock Cools Off — Is MU Now Too Cheap to Ignore?
news.Content: After a powerful rally over the past year, Micron (MU) stock has recently lost some momentum, declining 32% from its 52-week high of $471.34. The pullback reflects profit-taking by investors following the strong run-up, as well as emerging concerns about potential shifts in demand in the memory market. The recent uncertainty stems from developments in the artificial intelligence (AI) ecosystem. Alphabet (GOOGL) recently introduced TurboQuant, which lowers the memory requirements of AI models. Since memory and storage capacity are critical ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron (MU) stock has declined 32% from its 52-week high due to profit-taking and concerns about AI developments like Google's TurboQuant algorithm.
• TurboQuant could lower memory requirements for AI models, potentially slowing memory hardware demand growth.
• Despite this, the broader outlook for Micron remains constructive, with strong long-term demand expected for its memory and storage technologies.
• Micron delivered record Q2 revenue of $23.9 billion (up 75% sequentially, 196% YoY), driven by DRAM and NAND segments.
• The recent pullback has made Micron's valuation too cheap to ignore, with consolidated gross margin reaching 75%.
news.Keywords: Micron, AI, memory, valuation, earnings

news.Title: Prediction: Elon Musk Will Merge Tesla With SpaceX Within 5 Years
news.Content: Two of Elon Musk's companies, SpaceX and xAI, are merging. The combined entity will create, as Musk puts it, an "innovation engine" that combines many different growth opportunities together. In addition to artificial intelligence (AI), including xAI's Grok chatbot, the combined entity will also focus on rockets and space-based internet. Electric vehicles (EVs) are not part of the equation just yet. For now, Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) isn't involved in a merger with these businesses, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Elon Musk's companies SpaceX and xAI are merging to create an 'innovation engine' focused on AI, rockets, and space-based internet.
• Tesla is not currently involved in the merger but could be added in the future, potentially within five years.
• A merger would allow Musk to consolidate efforts and unify goals, exemplified by the Terafab project for a massive chip factory.
• If a merger occurs, Tesla's stock, already trading at a high valuation (over 300x trailing earnings), might see initial excitement but not necessarily sustainable gains.
• A wait-and-see approach is recommended for Tesla due to valuation concerns and uncertainties surrounding potential merged entity financials.
news.Keywords: Tesla, SpaceX, xAI, merger, valuation

news.Title: Amazon Just Lost a Key AI Chip Executive. Is That Bad News for AMZN Stock?
news.Content: Amazon.com (AMZN) has long been the everything store, powering online shopping, logistics, and, increasingly, the backbone of the internet through Amazon Web Services (AWS). But in today’s artificial intelligence (AI) era, the cloud edge depends heavily on chips. The ability to design powerful, cost-efficient AI hardware has become a defining advantage, and Amazon has been pushing hard with Amazon Trainium, its in-house accelerators built for training and running large AI models. Trainium has been gaining serious traction, especially with heavy usage from Anthropic’s Claude models, putting Amazon firmly in the AI infrastructure game. Behind that momentum was Gadi Hutt, a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon lost a key AI chip executive, Gadi Hutt, from Annapurna Labs, marking the second senior departure from the semiconductor startup.
• Amazon has been pushing its in-house accelerators, Amazon Trainium, for AI model training, gaining traction with Anthropic's Claude models.
• The stock has slipped 12.9% year-to-date, reflecting investor caution around Amazon's aggressive spending and margin pressure.
• Investors digested plans to pour $200 billion into AI, cloud infrastructure, robotics, and data centers, raising concerns about near-term profitability.
• Technical indicators like RSI and MACD suggest weakening momentum and rising selling pressure for AMZN stock.
news.Keywords: Amazon, AI chips, executive departure, spending, stock momentum
